initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['review']
Prompt family: review
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Write a tentative answer and request a reviewer child to perform a rigorous technical audit of the proposed solution. The reviewer must specifically check for constraint satisfaction, edge case vulnerabilities, and algorithmic efficiency. Ensure the reviewer provides actionable corrections to any identified flaws.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "review",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Write a tentative answer and request a reviewer child to perform a rigorous technical audit of the proposed solution. The reviewer must specifically check for constraint satisfaction, edge case vulnerabilities, and algorithmic efficiency. Ensure the reviewer provides actionable corrections to any identified flaws.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "review",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S3-ahc024-topological-map
Title: S3. AHC024 / Topological Map

Problem:
Compress a colored city map onto a 50 x 50 grid while preserving all color-adjacency relations and connectivity constraints, and maximize empty space.

Grading target:
E + 1, where E is the number of empty cells.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
final_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 2
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['execute_locally']
Prompt family: review
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
A review child has already returned critique or an improved draft. Use that returned work to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
A review child has already returned critique or an improved draft. Use that returned work to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}

Child results already returned to you:
[
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_01",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a constrained greedy packing algorithm using Disjoint Set Union (DSU) for connectivity and a BFS-based adjacency checker. 1. Graph Representation: Model color regions as nodes and adjacencies as edges. 2. Seed-and-Expand Strategy: Place large regions first in compact clusters (e.g., squares) to minimize perimeter. 3. Constraint Satisfaction: Use a look-ahead mechanism to ensure placing a cell does not isolate a required neighbor or violate the 50x50 boundary. 4. Deadlock Prevention: For fragmented maps, use a backtracking search with pruning to reorder placement when local density prevents satisfying future adjacencies. 5. Optimization: Maximize E by minimizing the total perimeter of all color clusters.\nJUSTIFICATION: Compact clustering minimizes perimeter, maximizing empty cells while preserving adjacencies."
  }
]
